question_text: Why does an electromagnet stop working immediately when you switch off the current, but a permanent magnet continues to work?
correct_answer: An electromagnet stops working immediately when the current is switched off because the magnetic field is created by the flowing electric current. Without current, there is no magnetic field. A permanent magnet continues to work because its electrons are permanently aligned in its atomic structure, so no external energy source is needed to maintain the magnetic field.
explanation: Electromagnets and permanent magnets work in different ways. In an electromagnet, electric current flowing through a coil creates a temporary magnetic field by aligning electrons. When you switch off the current, the electrons stop aligning and the magnetic field disappears instantly. In a permanent magnet, the electrons in the material are naturally and permanently aligned due to the atomic structure, so the magnetic field is always present without needing any external current.
